claims.topic: All Life Paths Have Equal Suffering; Outcome Differs by Choice
claims.text: Alex Hormozi asserts that suffering is a fixed cost incurred on every life path regardless of career choice, marital status, or wealth level; poor people suffer, rich people suffer, entrepreneurs suffer, employees suffer equally. The variable available to each person is not whether they suffer but what they receive in exchange for that suffering. Therefore the highest-leverage decision is selecting a goal large enough that the fixed suffering cost feels justified by the return.
claims.mechanism: Suffering is structurally invariant across life paths; the only degree of freedom is the magnitude and meaningfulness of the goal, which determines whether the fixed suffering cost yields a sufficient return.

claims.text: Alex Hormozi claims that humans uniquely apply the transitive property across domains (if A=B and B=C then A=C), which means success in any single domain can be generalized to other domains because the underlying skill-acquisition mechanism is domain-agnostic. A person who worked hard and succeeded in one subject, sport, or context can apply that same work ethic and belief to any new domain.
claims.mechanism: The transitive property of learning means that demonstrated ability to acquire any skill provides evidence of the meta-skill of skill acquisition itself, which transfers across all domains.
